config.factorsHint: Comma-separated numbers from 1–10. Leave empty = all.

disclaimer.title: ⚠️ Mystical Warning ⚠️
disclaimer.body:
Whosoever passes through this gate enters the realm of ancient numbers. The mystical cat Numerica guards five secret codes that unlock a surprise in the real world. These codes appear only to the worthy – and they must be noted by hand, in writing. No screenshot, no photo. Only ink on paper.

The dangers are real. Your mind will be tested. Your pen must be ready.

Are you prepared for this trial?
disclaimer.codesHint: 🔮 5 secret codes · 🐱 guarded by Numerica · ✍️ write them by hand
disclaimer.accept: I am ready. I accept the dangers.

code.title: 🌟 Secret Code Unlocked! 🌟
code.body: The mystical cat Numerica nods in approval. You have earned this code:
code.writeItDown: ✍️ Write this code on PAPER right NOW! It is not saved and will vanish shortly.
code.continue: I have written it down – continue!

redeemCodes.title: 🔐 Redeem Secret Codes
redeemCodes.subtitle: Enter all 5 secret codes in any order.
redeemCodes.wrong: ❌ Not quite right. Check your codes!
redeemCodes.masterCodeParentInstruction: 🎁 Show this master code to your parents – you've earned a surprise!

itHardwareQuiz.title: IT Hardware Quiz
itHardwareQuiz.description: Test your knowledge of computer hardware!

q.ap-q1: What is an Access Point used for?
q.ap-q2: Where is an Access Point typically used?
q.ap-a1-1: It provides a wireless Wi-Fi network
q.ap-a1-3: It displays images on the monitor
q.ap-a1-5: It processes calculations in the computer
q.ap-a2-1: In schools, offices or homes to distribute Wi-Fi
q.ap-a2-2: In the printer to store ink
q.ap-a2-3: In the monitor to mix colours
q.ap-a2-4: In the keyboard to illuminate keys
q.ap-a2-5: In the power supply to generate electricity
q.ssd-q2: What are the advantages of an SSD over an HDD?
q.ssd-q3: What does the abbreviation SSD stand for?
q.ssd-a1-1: It stores data permanently without moving parts
q.ssd-a1-2: It displays videos on the screen
q.ssd-a1-4: It connects computers to the internet
q.ssd-a2-2: It is quieter because no parts spin
q.sw-q1: What is a network switch used for?
q.sw-q2: What is the difference between a switch and a router?
q.sw-a1-1: It connects multiple devices within a network
q.sw-a1-4: It displays images on the screen
q.sw-a1-5: It supplies power to the computer
q.sw-a2-1: A switch connects devices within the same network; a router connects networks together
q.sw-a2-2: A switch is larger than a router
q.sw-a2-3: A switch always has more ports than a router
q.sw-a2-4: A router cannot send Wi-Fi signals
q.sw-a2-5: There is no difference, both do the same thing
q.psu-q1: What does the power supply do in a computer?
q.psu-q2: What happens when the power supply fails?
q.psu-a1-1: It converts the electricity from the socket into a form the computer can use
q.psu-a1-3: It displays images on the monitor
q.psu-a2-1: The computer receives no more power and shuts down
q.psu-a2-2: The computer automatically saves all data
q.mon-q2: What does 'Full HD' mean for a monitor?
q.mon-a1-1: It displays images, videos and programs from the computer
q.mon-a1-3: It connects the computer to the internet
q.mon-a2-1: The monitor displays images with 1920×1080 pixels
q.mon-a2-2: The monitor is made entirely of glass
q.mon-a2-3: The monitor can only show black and white
q.mon-a2-4: The monitor has a built-in speaker
q.kb-q1: What is a keyboard mainly used for?
q.kb-q2: What can you do with a keyboard?
q.kb-a1-1: To type text and commands into the computer
q.kb-a2-2: Use shortcuts like Ctrl+C to copy
q.ms-q1: What is a computer mouse used for?
q.ms-q2: What does a double-click with the left mouse button do?
q.ms-a1-1: To move the cursor and click on the screen
q.ms-a2-1: It opens a program or file
q.ms-a2-5: It connects the device to the internet
q.rt-q1: What is the main job of a router?
q.rt-q2: What does a Wi-Fi router transmit?
q.rt-a1-1: It connects the home network to the internet
q.rt-a1-2: It permanently stores photos and music
q.rt-a1-3: It displays programs on the screen
q.rt-a2-1: A radio signal that allows devices to connect wirelessly
q.rt-a2-2: Infrared light to control the computer
q.cpu-q1: What is the job of the processor (CPU)?
q.cpu-q2: What does the abbreviation CPU stand for?
q.cpu-q3: What affects the speed of a processor?
q.cpu-a1-1: It processes all calculations and runs programs
q.cpu-a1-4: It displays images on the monitor
q.ram-q2: What happens to the contents of RAM when you turn off the computer?
q.ram-q3: What does the abbreviation RAM stand for?
q.ram-a1-1: Data that is currently being used – but only temporarily
q.ram-a1-2: All files permanently, even when the computer is off
q.ram-a2-1: The data is lost because RAM is not permanent storage
q.ram-a2-3: The data is automatically copied to a USB stick
q.ram-a2-4: The data is uploaded to the cloud
q.ram-a2-5: The data is saved to the hard drive
q.gpu-q1: What is the graphics card (GPU) responsible for?
q.gpu-q2: Who benefits most from a powerful graphics card?
q.gpu-a1-1: It calculates and outputs images and videos on the monitor
q.gpu-a1-3: It supplies power to the computer
q.gpu-a2-3: Only people who print a lot
q.gpu-a2-4: Only people who use no programs
q.gpu-a2-5: Nobody, the CPU is always enough
q.usb-q1: What is a USB stick used for?
q.usb-q2: How do you connect a USB stick to a computer?
q.usb-a1-2: To connect the computer to the internet
q.usb-a1-3: To display images on the monitor
q.usb-a2-1: You plug it into a USB port
q.usb-a2-2: You put it in the CD drive
q.usb-a2-4: You screw it into the graphics card
q.usb-a2-5: You attach it to the monitor
q.wc-q1: What is a webcam mainly used for?
q.wc-a1-1: For video calls and online lessons
q.wc-a2-1: Live images of people in front of the computer
q.pr-q2: Which two types of printer are most common?
q.pr-a1-1: It transfers documents and images onto paper
q.pr-a1-3: It displays images on a screen
q.pr-a1-4: It connects devices in a network
q.hs-q2: What is a headset mostly used for?
q.hs-a1-4: A cable adapter for the monitor
q.hs-a2-1: For gaming, listening to music and video calls
q.hdd-q1: What does a hard drive (HDD) do?
q.hdd-q2: What are disadvantages of an HDD compared to an SSD?
q.hdd-a1-1: It stores data permanently on spinning disks
q.hdd-a1-2: It displays images on the monitor
q.hdd-a1-3: It connects the computer to Wi-Fi
q.hdd-a2-1: It is slower at reading and writing
q.hdd-a2-2: It is more sensitive to shocks because it has moving parts
q.hdd-a2-5: It is always more expensive than an SSD
q.sp-q2: What is the difference between a speaker and a headset?
q.sp-a1-1: It outputs sounds, music and speech audibly
q.sp-a1-2: It displays images on the screen
q.sp-a2-1: A speaker plays sound for everyone; a headset only for the person wearing it
q.sp-a2-2: A speaker always has more bass than a headset
q.sp-a2-4: A speaker always needs a Bluetooth connection
q.sm-q2: What can a smartphone do that a basic mobile phone cannot?
q.sm-a1-1: A mobile phone with a touchscreen and many app functions
q.sm-a1-3: A device only for calling without internet access
q.sm-a1-4: A kind of tablet without a camera
q.sm-a1-5: A wearable worn on the wrist
q.tb-q2: What advantage does a tablet have over a laptop?
q.tb-a1-1: A flat computer with a touchscreen, usually without a built-in keyboard
q.tb-a1-2: A laptop with an especially thin screen
q.tb-a1-5: An input device for graphic designers
q.tb-a2-1: It is lighter and can be operated by touchscreen
q.tb-a2-2: It always has more computing power
q.tb-a2-4: It is easier to program on
q.tb-a2-5: It always has a better camera
q.sd-q1: What is an SD card used for?
q.sd-q2: What does SD stand for in SD card?
q.sd-a1-1: Storing data in cameras, smartphones or tablets
q.uh-q1: What does a USB hub do?
q.uh-q2: When do you need a USB hub?
q.uh-a1-1: It expands one USB port into multiple ports at once
q.uh-a1-3: It connects the computer to the internet
q.uh-a2-1: When you want to connect more USB devices than the computer has ports
q.uh-a2-2: When the internet connection is too slow
q.uh-a2-3: When the screen is too small
q.uh-a2-5: When you want to repair a device
q.eh-q1: What is an external hard drive?
q.eh-q2: What is an external hard drive especially useful for?
q.eh-a1-1: A portable storage device that connects to the computer via USB
q.eh-a1-2: A device that can only be installed inside the computer
q.eh-a1-3: A Wi-Fi storage device for the home network
q.eh-a2-1: As a backup of important files
q.eh-a2-2: For taking large amounts of data with you
q.gp-q1: What is a gamepad (controller) used for?
q.gp-q2: On which devices can you use a controller?
q.gp-a1-1: To control characters and actions in video games
q.gp-a1-2: To type text on the computer
q.gp-a2-1: On gaming consoles, PCs and smartphones
q.gp-a2-5: Only on TVs without a gaming console
q.pb-q2: How can you tell how much energy a power bank can store?
q.pb-a1-1: A portable battery that can charge smartphones and other devices on the go
q.pb-a1-3: A device for measuring battery performance
q.pb-a1-4: A USB hub with its own power
q.pb-a2-1: By the capacity rating in milliampere-hours (mAh)
q.pb-a2-3: By the number of USB ports
q.pb-a2-4: By the colour of the casing
q.pb-a2-5: By the size of the display
q.rpi-q1: What is a single-board computer (SBC) like the Raspberry Pi?
q.rpi-q2: What can you do with a Raspberry Pi?
q.rpi-q3: What else do you need to use a Raspberry Pi?
q.rpi-a1-1: A very small and affordable computer on a single circuit board
q.rpi-a1-3: A mini router for the home network
q.rpi-a1-4: A small USB stick with an operating system
q.rpi-a2-1: Learn to program, control robots or build a mini server
q.rpi-a3-1: A power supply (e.g. USB-C power adapter)
q.rpi-a3-2: An SD card with an operating system
q.mic-q1: What is an external microphone used for?
q.mic-q2: Who benefits most from an external microphone?
q.mic-a1-1: Recording speech or music with better quality than a built-in microphone
q.vr-a1-1: A device you wear over your eyes that shows a three-dimensional virtual world
q.vr-a1-2: Protective goggles for working at the computer
